===''Pokémon the Series''=== [[File:Viola anime.png|thumb|250px|Viola in {{aniseries|XY}}]] ===={{aniseries|BW}}==== Viola was first mentioned in ''[[BW142|The Dream Continues!]]'', albeit not by name, when [[Alexa]] revealed to {{Ash}} that her sister was a Gym Leader from the Kalos region. ===={{aniseries|XY}}==== When Ash arrived in Kalos in ''[[XY001|Kalos, Where Dreams and Adventures Begin!]]'', he learned that Viola was absent from her Gym at Santalune City. However, Ash decided to head to [[Santalune Gym]] anyway after his challenge at [[Lumiose Gym]] was declined. [[XY005|He arrived there]] just as Viola, who was revealed to a professional photographer known for her excellent shots of Bug-type Pokémon, was returning and she accepted Ash's challenge. Viola's {{p|Surskit}} and {{p|Vivillon}} had a type disadvantage against [[Ash's Fletchling]] and {{AP|Pikachu}}, however Viola's strategy was to immobilize both Pokémon using {{m|Ice Beam}} and {{m|Sticky Web}}. This worked and Ash lost the battle. In ''[[XY006|Battling on Thin Ice!]]'', Ash challenged Viola to a [[rematch]]. Thanks to the training Ash had with {{ashfr}} between the battles, he was able to counter Viola's strategies and defeat her, winning himself the {{badge|Bug}}. She later reappeared in a flashback in ''[[XY012|To Catch a Pokémon Smuggler!]]''. In ''[[XY020|Breaking Titles at the Chateau!]]'', Viola reunited with Ash and his friends at the [[Battle Chateau]]. There, she and Surskit battled [[Grant]] and his {{p|Onix}}, and lost. In ''[[XY112|Master Class is in Session!]]'' and ''[[XY113|Performing a Pathway to the Future!]]'', Viola watched {{an|Serena}}'s Master Class [[Pokémon Showcase]] along with Alexa. She appeared in a fantasy in ''[[XY125|A League of His Own!]]'', when Ash was talking to [[Everett]] about the Gym Leaders that he had defeated in Kalos, in order for him to be able to compete in the [[Lumiose Conference]]. In ''[[XY132|A Towering Takeover!]]'', Viola was seen watching [[Lysandre]]'s broadcast from Lumiose City along with Alexa. In ''[[XY135|Rocking Kalos Defenses!]]'' and ''[[XY136|Forming a More Perfect Union!]]'', Viola and Vivillon were among those who helped destroy the [[Giant Rock]]. She appeared again in flashbacks in ''[[XY140|Till We Compete Again!]]''. ====Character==== Like her sister, Viola is very cheerful and friendly but has an unwavering focus in battle. According to Alexa, her trips away from the Santalune Gym often last for some time. She helps Alexa with her work at times and she shares a friendship with Grant, though she is shown to get easily annoyed whenever Grant falls from the walls he is climbing on. ====Pokémon==== {{MissingInfo|1|java|scope=section}} This listing is of Viola's known {{OBP|Pokémon|species}} in {{aniseries|PTS}}: {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Viola |pkmn=Surskit |type1=Bug |type2=Water |img=Viola Surskit.png |epnum=XY005 |epname=A Blustery Santalune Gym Battle! |vajp= |vaen=Eileen Stevens |desc={{p|Surskit}} is Viola's first known Pokémon. It was first used against [[Ash's Pikachu]] in their first Gym battle. Surskit defeated Pikachu by freezing the battlefield with Ice Beam, which prevented him from moving properly and left him unable to escape Surskit's Signal Beam. Next, it fought {{AP|Fletchling}}, who quickly defeated Surskit due to its ability to fly. In ''[[XY006|Battling on Thin Ice!]]'', Surskit battled Pikachu again in a rematch, where its previous tactics failed due to Pikachu using his tail to balance himself on the ice. It was eventually defeated by Pikachu's {{m|Thunderbolt}}. Surskit reappeared in ''[[XY020|Breaking Titles at the Chateau!]]'', where it battled [[Grant]]'s {{p|Onix}} but lost. It appeared in flashbacks in ''[[XY021|A PokeVision of Things to Come!]]'' and ''[[XY023|Coming Back into the Cold]]''. Surskit's known moves are {{m|Protect}}, {{m|Ice Beam}}, {{m|Signal Beam}}, {{m|Sticky Web}}, and {{m|Bubble Beam}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Viola |pkmn=Vivillon |type1=Bug |type2=Flying |img=Viola Vivillon.png |epnum=XY005 |epname=A Blustery Santalune Gym Battle! |vajp=Aya Endō |vaen=Eileen Stevens |desc={{p|Vivillon}} is Viola's second known Pokémon. It was first used against Ash in their first Gym battle. It was sent out to battle {{AP|Fletchling}} after it defeated Surskit. It won the battle by using Psychic to trap Fletchling in Surskit's {{m|Sticky Web}}, leaving it defenseless to Vivillon's Solar Beam. In ''[[XY006|Battling on Thin Ice!]]'', Vivillon faced Fletchling again in a rematch, where it beat Fletchling again by hitting it with Sleep Powder, leaving it defenseless to Solar Beam once more. Then it fought {{AP|Pikachu}}, where Vivillon was defeated after its wing was frozen by the ice surrounding the Gym, hindering its ability to fly and leaving it an easy target for Pikachu's {{m|Thunderbolt}}. It reappeared in a flashback in ''[[XY012|To Catch a Pokemon Smuggler!]]''. It appeared again in ''[[XY135|Rocking Kalos Defenses!]]'' and ''[[XY136|Forming a More Perfect Union!]]'', where it helped destroy the [[Giant Rock]]. It reappeared in a flashback in ''[[XY140|Till We Compete Again!]]''. Vivillon's known moves are {{m|Psychic}}, {{m|Gust}}, {{m|Solar Beam}}, and {{m|Sleep Powder}}.}} ====Achievements==== [[File:Viola Duchess anime.png|thumb|250px|Viola as a Duchess]] =====Battle Chateau===== Viola achieved the following [[Battle Chateau#Ranks|ranks]] in [[Battle Chateau]]: * {{tc|Duchess}} (prior to ''[[XY020|Breaking Titles at the Chateau!]]'') ====Voice actors==== {{vatable|color={{bug color light}}|bordercolor={{bug color}} |ja=下屋則子 ''[[Noriko Shitaya]]'' |en=[[Lori Gardner]] |fi=Henni-Liisa Stam ([[XY005]]-[[XY006]], [[XY020]])
